description: Miscellaneous operating system interfaces. Provides a portable way of using operating system dependent functionality such as reading or writing to the file system, starting processes, and working with environment variables.

functions.name: getcwd

returns.description: A string representing the current working directory

functions.description: Return a string representing the current working directory.

functions.name: listdir

params.name: path
params.description: Path to the directory to list

returns.description: List of names of entries in the directory (strings), excluding '.' and '..'

functions.description: Return a list containing the names of the entries in the directory given by path. The list is in arbitrary order and does not include the special entries '.' and '..'.

functions.name: mkdir

params.name: path
params.description: Path of the directory to create

params.name: mode
params.description: Numeric mode for the directory permissions (default 0777). On some systems, mode is ignored.

functions.description: Create a directory named path with numeric mode mode. Raises OSError if the directory already exists.

functions.name: makedirs

params.name: path
params.description: Path of the directory to create, including any intermediate directories

params.name: mode
params.description: Numeric mode for the directory permissions (default 0777)

functions.description: Recursive directory creation function. Like mkdir(), but makes all intermediate-level directories needed to contain the leaf directory.

functions.name: remove

params.name: path
params.description: Path to the file to remove

functions.description: Remove (delete) the file at path. If path is a directory, OSError is raised. Use rmdir() to remove directories.

functions.name: removedirs

params.name: path
params.description: Path of directory tree to remove

functions.description: Remove directories recursively. Works like rmdir() except that, if the leaf directory is successfully removed, directories corresponding to rightmost path segments will be pruned away until either the whole path is consumed or an error is raised.

functions.name: rename

params.name: src
params.description: Current path of the file or directory

params.name: dst
params.description: New path of the file or directory

functions.description: Rename the file or directory src to dst. If dst is a directory, OSError will be raised. On Unix, if dst exists and is a file, it will be replaced silently if the user has permission.

functions.name: rmdir

params.name: path
params.description: Path of the directory to remove

functions.description: Remove (delete) the directory path. Only works when the directory is empty, otherwise OSError is raised.

functions.name: stat

params.name: path
params.description: Path to the file or directory

returns.description: A stat_result object with attributes st_mode, st_ino, st_dev, st_nlink, st_uid, st_gid, st_size, st_atime, st_mtime, st_ctime

functions.description: Perform a stat system call on the given path. Return a stat_result object with information about the file.

functions.name: walk

params.name: top
params.description: Root directory to walk

params.name: topdown
params.description: If True (default), the triple for a directory is generated before the triples for any of its subdirectories. If False, bottom-up.

params.name: onerror
params.description: A function called with one argument, an OSError instance, for each error. By default, errors are ignored.

params.name: followlinks
params.description: If True, follow symbolic links. Default is False.

returns.description: Generator yielding 3-tuples (dirpath, dirnames, filenames) for each directory in the tree rooted at top

functions.description: Generate the file names in a directory tree by walking the tree either top-down or bottom-up. For each directory in the tree, yields a 3-tuple (dirpath, dirnames, filenames).

functions.name: getenv

params.name: varname
params.description: The environment variable name

params.name: value
params.description: Default value to return if the variable is not set (default None)

returns.description: The value of the environment variable, or the default value if not set

functions.description: Return the value of the environment variable varname if it exists, or value if it doesn't. value defaults to None.

functions.name: putenv

params.name: varname
params.description: The environment variable name

params.name: value
params.description: The value to set

functions.description: Set the environment variable named varname to the string value. Such changes to the environment affect subprocesses started with os.system(), popen(), or fork() and execv(). Note: on some platforms, os.environ is not updated.

functions.name: system

params.name: command
params.description: The command string to execute in a subshell

returns.description: The exit status of the process (platform dependent)

functions.description: Execute the command (a string) in a subshell. On Unix, the return value is the exit status of the process encoded in the format specified for wait().

functions.name: popen

params.name: command
params.description: The command string to execute

params.name: mode
params.description: Either 'r' (default) to read stdout, or 'w' to write to stdin of the command

params.name: bufsize
params.description: Buffer size argument for the resulting file object (same meaning as in open())

returns.description: An open file object connected to the pipe

functions.description: Open a pipe to or from command. The return value is an open file object connected to the pipe, which can be read or written depending on whether mode is 'r' (default) or 'w'.

functions.name: chmod

params.name: path
params.description: Path to the file or directory

params.name: mode
params.description: Numeric mode (e.g., 0755)

functions.description: Change the mode of path to the numeric mode.

functions.name: chown

params.name: path
params.description: Path to the file or directory

params.name: uid
params.description: Numeric user ID. Set to -1 to leave unchanged.

params.name: gid
params.description: Numeric group ID. Set to -1 to leave unchanged.

functions.description: Change the owner and group id of path to the numeric uid and gid. Available on Unix only.

functions.name: getpid
functions.description: Return the current process ID.

functions.name: getppid
functions.description: Return the parent's process ID. On Unix, when the parent process has exited, the id returned is the one of the init process (1).

functions.name: kill

params.name: pid
params.description: Process ID to send the signal to

params.name: sig
params.description: Signal number to send (e.g., signal.SIGTERM, signal.SIGKILL)

functions.description: Send signal sig to the process pid. Constants for the specific signals available on the host platform are defined in the signal module.

functions.name: urandom

params.name: n
params.description: Number of random bytes to return

returns.description: A string of n random bytes suitable for cryptographic use

functions.description: Return a string of n random bytes suitable for cryptographic use. This function returns random bytes from an OS-specific randomness source.

constants.name: sep
constants.description: The character used by the operating system to separate pathname components. This is '/' for POSIX and '\\' for Windows.

constants.name: linesep
constants.description: The string used to separate lines on the current platform. This is '\n' on POSIX and '\r\n' on Windows.

constants.name: curdir
constants.description: The constant string used by the operating system to refer to the current directory. This is '.' for POSIX and Windows.

constants.name: pardir
constants.description: The constant string used by the operating system to refer to the parent directory. This is '..' for POSIX and Windows.

constants.name: devnull
constants.description: The file path of the null device. '/dev/null' for POSIX, 'nul' for Windows.

constants.name: name
constants.description: The name of the operating system dependent module imported: 'posix', 'nt', 'os2', 'ce', 'java', 'riscos'.

constants.name: extsep
constants.description: The character which separates the base filename from the extension. This is '.' on most platforms.

constants.name: environ
constants.description: A mapping object representing the string environment. Modifying this mapping will update the environment for the current process and any child processes.

constants.name: path
constants.description: The os.path submodule providing common pathname manipulations. Contains functions like os.path.join(), os.path.exists(), os.path.basename(), os.path.dirname(), os.path.splitext(), os.path.isfile(), os.path.isdir(), os.path.abspath(), os.path.getsize().
